claims.claim: Hebrew-calendar holiday Gregorian dates used in content_planner.py, verified against hebcal.com. Yom HaShoah: 2025-04-24, 2026-04-14, 2027-05-04. 2026: Purim 03-03, Pesach 04-02, Yom HaZikaron 04-21, Yom Ha'atzmaut 04-22, Shavuot 05-22, Rosh Hashana 09-12, Yom Kippur 09-21, Sukkot 09-26, Hanukkah 12-05. 2027: Purim 03-23, Pesach 04-22, Yom HaZikaron 05-11, Yom Ha'atzmaut 05-12, Shavuot 06-11, Rosh Hashana 10-02, Yom Kippur 10-11, Sukkot 10-16, Hanukkah 12-25 (first full day). 2025 Yom HaZikaron is 2025-04-30 and Yom Ha'atzmaut 2025-05-01.
claims.raw_snippet: Hebcal: Yom HaShoah 2025-04-24, 2026-04-14, 2027-05-04. 2027: Purim 2027-03-23, Pesach I 2027-04-22, Yom HaZikaron 2027-05-11, Yom HaAtzma'ut 2027-05-12, Shavuot 2027-06-11, Rosh Hashana 5788 2027-10-02, Yom Kippur 2027-10-11, Sukkot I 2027-10-16, Chanukah 1 candle 2027-12-24 (first full day 12-25). 2025 Yom HaZikaron 2025-04-30, Yom HaAtzma'ut 2025-05-01.

claims.claim: Israeli tech/business media operating in 2026: Geektime (Hebrew tech/startups), Calcalist + Calcalist Tech (Hebrew business+tech) with CTech as its English edition, The Marker (Hebrew business, Haaretz group), Globes (Hebrew financial daily), Ynet Tech (general), No Camels (English, Israeli innovation, non-profit at Reichman University).
claims.raw_snippet: Geektime, Calcalist/Calcalist Tech, CTech (Calcalist English edition), The Marker, Globes, Ynet Tech, and No Camels are active Israeli tech/business outlets in 2026.

claims.claim: AI answer engines (AI Overviews, LLM answer engines) cite earned media (news, third-party guides, reputable directories) far more than brand-owned sites; ~82% of AI citations are earned media. Question-to-answer-block structure, tables, and schema markup are the documented levers, and AEO is the biggest 2026 content shift.
claims.raw_snippet: Roughly 82% of AI citations come from earned media; AI Overviews average ~13 sources per response in 2026 and reward answer-block structure and schema.

claims.claim: Israeli content consumption is heavily mobile-first (over 70%); Israel has ~91% smartphone penetration and >100% mobile connections per capita, so >70% is a conservative floor.
claims.raw_snippet: Israel: ~91% smartphone penetration, mobile connections exceed the population; mobile-first consumption well above 70%.

claims.claim: Israel's anti-spam law (Chok HaSpam, Amendment 40 to the Communications Law) governs commercial email/SMS; the Israeli work week is Sunday-Thursday.
claims.raw_snippet: חוק הספאם (תיקון 40 לחוק התקשורת) אוסר משלוח דבר פרסומת ללא הסכמה מפורשת מראש של הנמען; שבוע העבודה בישראל הוא ראשון עד חמישי.
